devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । भवो भवान्या निधनं प्रजापतेः । असत्कृताया अवगम्य नारदात् । स्वपार्षदसैन्यं च तदध्वरर्भुभिः । विद्रावितं क्रोधमपारमादधे ॥ १ ...
english_devnagari: maitreya uvāca bhavo bhavānyā nidhanaṃ prajāpateḥ asatkṛtāyā avagamya nāradāt svapārṣadasainyaṃ ca tadadhvararbhubhiḥ vidrāvitaṃ krodhamapāramādadhe

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Maitreya said:
Having learnt from Nārada, the report of the death of his wife (Satī) on being insulted by Prajāpati Dakṣa, and of the total rout of the army of his attendants by the Ṛbhus in that Sacrifice, Śiva’s anger knew no bounds.

devanagari: क्रुद्धः सुदष्टौष्ठपुटः स धूर्जटिः । जटां तडिद् वह्निसटोग्ररोचिषम् । उत्कृत्य रुद्रः सहसोत्थितो हसन् । गम्भीरनादो विससर्ज तां भुवि ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: kruddhaḥ sudaṣṭauṣṭhapuṭaḥ sa dhūrjaṭiḥ jaṭāṃ taḍid vahnisaṭograrociṣam utkṛtya rudraḥ sahasotthito hasan gambhīranādo visasarja tāṃ bhuvi

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ has class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ has class 1 verb]

translation: Śiva (the god with matted locks of hair) got enraged, and bit his lips. He pulled out a matted lock of hair which dazzled with terrific splendour, like the flash of lightning, and a flame of fire. He suddenly sprang to his feet, and with a loud laughter and roar, dashed it on the ground.

devanagari: ततोऽतिकायस्तनुवा स्पृशन्दिवं । सहस्रबाहुर्घनरुक् त्रिसूर्यदृक् । करालदंष्ट्रो ज्वलदग्निमूर्धजः । कपालमाली विविधोद्यतायुधः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: tato sahasrabāhurghanaruk trisūryadṛk karāladaṃṣṭro jvaladagnimūrdhajaḥ kapālamālī vividhodyatāyudhaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ spṛś class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ spṛś class 6 verb]
[nominative single from √ jval class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ jval class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ jval class 1 verb]

translation: From it (was born Vīrabhadra), of colossal size, who touched the skies, by his stature. He had a thousand arms. His complexion was like a dark cloud. He had three eyes brilliant like the Sun. He had sharp fangs. His locks of hair were shining like burning flames of fire. He wore a garland of skulls and held various weapons aloft (for striking).

devanagari: तं किं करोमीति गृणन्तमाह । बद्धाञ्जलिं भगवान् भूतनाथः । दक्षं सयज्ञं जहि मद्‍भटानां । त्वमग्रणी रुद्र भटांशको मे ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ kiṃ karomīti gṛṇantamāha baddhāñjaliṃ bhagavān bhūtanāthaḥ dakṣaṃ sayajñaṃ jahi mad‍bhaṭānāṃ tvamagraṇī rudra bhaṭāṃśako me
translation: Śiva, the Supreme Lord, the master of bhūtas, commanded Vīrabhadra who, with folded hands, requested for an order. “Oh Rudra, as you are my part (manifestation, you need not be afraid of Brahmanic power). You be the leader of my army, oh warrior, and destroy Dakṣa along with his sacrifice”.

devanagari: आज्ञप्त एवं कुपितेन मन्युना । स देवदेवं परिचक्रमे विभुम् । मेनेतदात्मानमसङ्गरंहसा । महीयसां तात सहः सहिष्णुम् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: ājñapta evaṃ kupitena manyunā sa devadevaṃ paricakrame vibhum menetadātmānamasaṅgaraṃhasā mahīyasāṃ tāta sahaḥ sahiṣṇum

translation: Being thus commanded by wrathful Śiva, he circumambulated the all-powerful god of gods. Oh Vidura, he then regarded himself capable of withstanding the might of very powerful warriors, by his irresistible force.

devanagari: अन्वीयमानः स तु रुद्रपार्षदैः । भृशं नदद्भिर्व्यनदत्सुभैरवम् । उद्यम्य शूलं जगदन्तकान्तकं । स प्राद्रवद् घोषणभूषणाङ्घ्रिः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: anvīyamānaḥ sa tu rudrapārṣadaiḥ bhṛśaṃ nadadbhirvyanadatsubhairavam udyamya śūlaṃ jagadantakāntakaṃ sa prādravad ghoṣaṇabhūṣaṇāṅghriḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ prā class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ prā class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ prā class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ ru class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ ru class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ ru class 1 verb]

translation: He was followed by the attendants of Rudra who were roaring loudly. He himself thundered most terribly. Raising his trident which was capable of killing even the god of Death, he dashed forth, with his feet jingling with ornaments.

devanagari: अथर्त्विजो यजमानः सदस्याः । ककुभ्युदीच्यां प्रसमीक्ष्य रेणुम् । तमः किमेतत्कुत एतद्रजोऽभू । दिति द्विजा द्विजपत्‍न्यश्च दध्युः ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: athartvijo yajamānaḥ sadasyāḥ kakubhyudīcyāṃ prasamīkṣya reṇum tamaḥ kimetatkuta etadrajo diti dvijā dvijapat‍nyaśca dadhyuḥ
translation: Having seen (a cloud of) dust in the northern direction, the sacrificial priest, the sacrificer (Dakṣa), members of the sacrificial assembly, Brāhmaṇas and their wives began to speculate: “What darkness is this? From which place is the cloud of dust (coming)? Winds are not blowing. Nor can these be robbers, as king Prācīnabarhiṣ of iron rule, is still alive. Cows are not driven home so hastily. Whence is this dust? Is the world now due for dissolution (pralaya)?”

devanagari: वाता न वान्ति न हि सन्ति दस्यवः । प्राचीनबर्हिर्जीवति होग्रदण्डः । गावो न काल्यन्त इदं कुतो रजो । लोकोऽधुना किं प्रलयाय कल्पते ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: vātā na vānti na hi santi dasyavaḥ prācīnabarhirjīvati hogradaṇḍaḥ gāvo na kālyanta idaṃ kuto rajo loko

translation: Having seen (a cloud of) dust in the northern direction, the sacrificial priest, the sacrificer (Dakṣa), members of the sacrificial assembly, Brāhmaṇas and their wives began to speculate: “What darkness is this? From which place is the cloud of dust (coming)? Winds are not blowing. Nor can these be robbers, as king Prācīnabarhiṣ of iron rule, is still alive. Cows are not driven home so hastily. Whence is this dust? Is the world now due for dissolution (pralaya)?”

devanagari: प्रसूतिमिश्राः स्त्रिय उद्विग्नचित्ता । ऊचुर्विपाको वृजिनस्यैव तस्य । यत्पश्यन्तीनां दुहितॄणां प्रजेशः । सुतां सतीमवदध्यावनागाम् ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: prasūtimiśrāḥ striya udvignacittā ūcurvipāko vṛjinasyaiva tasya yatpaśyantīnāṃ duhitṝṇāṃ prajeśaḥ sutāṃ satīmavadadhyāvanāgām

translation: Ladies of whom Prasūti was prominent, commented with a troubled heart: “This is the consequence of the sin committed by Dakṣa, the Lord of Creation, who slighted his innocent daughter Satī, in the presence of his other daughters.

devanagari: यस्त्वन्तकाले व्युप्तजटाकलापः । स्वशूलसूच्यर्पितदिग्गजेन्द्रः । वितत्य नृत्यत्युदितास्त्रदोर्ध्वजान् । उच्चाट्टहास स्तनयित्‍नुभिन्नदिक् ...
english_devnagari: yastvantakāle vyuptajaṭākalāpaḥ svaśūlasūcyarpitadiggajendraḥ vitatya nṛtyatyuditāstradordhvajān uccāṭṭahāsa stanayit‍nubhinnadik

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ nṛt class 4 verb], [vocative dual from √ nṛt class 4 verb], [accusative dual from √ nṛt class 4 verb], [locative single from √ nṛt class 4 verb]

[nominative plural from √ vad class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ vad class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ vad class 1 verb]

translation: It may be due to Dakṣa’s offence against Rudra who dances at the end of the universe, with his locks of matted hair scattered about, and with the elephants supporting the directions pierced on the prongs of his trident, and with his bannerlike hands holding up the missiles (for discharge), tearing down the directions, by thunderlike peals of laughter.

devanagari: अमर्षयित्वा तमसह्यतेजसं । मन्युप्लुतं दुर्निरीक्ष्यं भ्रुकुट्या । करालदंष्ट्राभिरुदस्तभागणं । स्यात् स्वस्ति किं कोपयतो विधातुः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: amarṣayitvā tamasahyatejasaṃ manyuplutaṃ durnirīkṣyaṃ bhrukuṭyā karāladaṃṣṭrābhirudastabhāgaṇaṃ syāt svasti kiṃ kopayato vidhātuḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ plu class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ plu class 1 verb]
[accusative plural from √ kup class 10 verb], [ablative single from √ kup class 10 verb], [genitive single from √ kup class 10 verb]
[ablative single from √ kup class 10 verb], [genitive single from √ kup class 10 verb]

translation: When he is full of rage, can anyone fare well, even if he be god Brahmā himself who provoked his (Rudra’s) wrath—Rudra, who is of irresistible splendour, full of wrath, unbearable to look at when he knits his eyebrows in angry looks, and who has scattered the whole group of constellations with his terrible fangs.”

devanagari: बह्वेवमुद्विग्न दृशोच्यमाने । जनेन दक्षस्य मुहुर्महात्मनः । उत्पेतुरुत्पाततमाः सहस्रशो । भयावहा दिवि भूमौ च पर्यक् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: bahvevamudvigna dṛśocyamāne janena dakṣasya muhurmahātmanaḥ utpeturutpātatamāḥ sahasraśo bhayāvahā divi bhūmau ca paryak

[nominative dual from √ vac class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ vac class 2 verb], [vocative dual from √ vac class 2 verb], [accusative dual from √ vac class 2 verb], [nominative dual from √ vac class 3 verb], [vocative single from √ vac class 3 verb], [vocative dual from √ vac class 3 verb], [accusative dual from √ vac class 3 verb]

translation: While people with perturbed eyes, were speculating thus wildly, there were thousands of omens portending misfortune to Dakṣa, frequently appearing in the sky, and on the earth.

devanagari: तावत्स रुद्रानुचरैर्मखो महान् । नानायुधैर्वामनकैरुदायुधैः । पिङ्गैः पिशङ्गैर्मकरोदराननैः । पर्याद्रवद्भिः विदुरान्वरुध्यत ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: tāvatsa rudrānucarairmakho mahān nānāyudhairvāmanakairudāyudhaiḥ piṅgaiḥ piśaṅgairmakarodarānanaiḥ paryādravadbhiḥ vidurānvarudhyata
translation: Oh Vidura, in the meanwhile the big sacrificial pandal was rushed at on all sides, and occupied by the followers of Rudra with their weapons held up in their hands. They were short in stature, dark and brown (in complexion) and their faces and stomachs were like crocodiles.

devanagari: केचिद्‍बभञ्जुः प्राग्वंशं पत्‍नीशालां तथापरे । सद आग्नीध्रशालां च तद्विहारं महानसम् ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: kecid‍babhañjuḥ prāgvaṃśaṃ pat‍nīśālāṃ tathāpare sada āgnīdhraśālāṃ ca tadvihāraṃ mahānasam
translation: Some broke the Prāgvaṃśa (the beam resting on the eastern and western pillars of the sacrificial hall). Others broke the apartment of the sacrificer’s wife (This is to the west of the sacrificial hall). Some broke the assembly hall in front (of the sacrificial hall). Some broke the store-room of the materials for sacrificial offerings; others, the cottage of the sacrificer himself, and the kitchen.

devanagari: रुरुजुर्यज्ञपात्राणि तथैकेऽग्नीननाशयन् । कुण्डेष्वमूत्रयन्केचिद् बिभिदुर्वेदिमेखलाः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: rurujuryajñapātrāṇi tathaike kuṇḍeṣvamūtrayankecid bibhidurvedimekhalāḥ
translation: Some smashed the sacrificial utensils; some put out the sacrificial fire; some urinated in the sacrificial pit, and some cut down the zonelike construction around the sacrificial platform.

devanagari: अबाधन्त मुनीनन्ये एके पत्‍नीरतर्जयन् । अपरे जगृहुर्देवान् प्रत्यासन्नान् पलायितान् ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: abādhanta munīnanye eke pat‍nīratarjayan apare jagṛhurdevān pratyāsannān palāyitān
translation: Some struck the sages; others threatened the wives, of the priests, etc.; others seized gods who were near by and trying to run away.

devanagari: भृगुं बबन्ध मणिमान् वीरभद्रः प्रजापतिम् । चण्डेशः पूषणं देवं भगं नन्दीश्वरोऽग्रहीत् ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: bhṛguṃ babandha maṇimān vīrabhadraḥ prajāpatim caṇḍeśaḥ pūṣaṇaṃ devaṃ bhagaṃ nandīśvaro

translation: Maṇiman bound Bhṛgu; Vīrabhadra, Prajāpati Dakṣa; Caṇḍīśa, god Pūṣan; Nandīśvara caught hold of Bhaga.

devanagari: सर्व एवर्त्विजो दृष्ट्वा सदस्याः सदिवौकसः । तैरर्द्यमानाः सुभृशं ग्रावभिर्नैकधाद्रवन् ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: sarva evartvijo dṛṣṭvā sadasyāḥ sadivaukasaḥ tairardyamānāḥ subhṛśaṃ grāvabhirnaikadhādravan
translation: All the sacrificial priests, along with the members of the assembly and gods, saw this. Being severely pelted with, stones, they ran quickly, helter-skelter in all directions.

devanagari: जुह्वतः स्रुवहस्तस्य श्मश्रूणि भगवान्भवः । भृगोर्लुलुञ्चे सदसि योऽहसत् श्मश्रु दर्शयन् ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: juhvataḥ sruvahastasya śmaśrūṇi bhagavānbhavaḥ bhṛgorluluñce sadasi yo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ hu class 3 verb], [vocative plural from √ hu class 3 verb], [accusative plural from √ hu class 3 verb], [genitive single from √ hu class 3 verb]

[nominative single from √ dṛś ], [vocative single from √ dṛś ]

translation: Glorious Vīrabhadra pulled out mustaches and the beard of Bhṛgu, while he was offering the oblations with sruva (a ladle) in his hand, as he had laughed derisively in the assembly showing his beard (when Dakṣa insulted Śiva).

devanagari: भगस्य नेत्रे भगवान् पातितस्य रुषा भुवि । उज्जहार सदःस्थोऽक्ष्णा यः शपन्तं असूसुचत् ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: bhagasya netre bhagavān pātitasya ruṣā bhuvi ujjahāra sadaḥstho
translation: Out of anger, venerated Vīrabhadra knocked down Bhaga and pulled out his eyes, as in that sacrificial session of the assembly (while Dakṣa censured Śiva), he made signs with his eyes to Dakṣa.

devanagari: पूष्णो ह्यपातयद् दन्तान् कालिङ्गस्य यथा बलः । शप्यमाने गरिमणि योऽहसद् दर्शयन्दतः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: pūṣṇo hyapātayad dantān kāliṅgasya yathā balaḥ śapyamāne garimaṇi yo

translation: He knocked out the teeth of Pūṣan as Balarāma did to the king of Kaliṅga, as he laughed, showing his teeth, while the great god Śiva was vilified by Dakṣa.

devanagari: आक्रम्योरसि दक्षस्य शितधारेण हेतिना । छिन्दन्नपि तदुद्धर्तुं नाशक्नोत् त्र्यम्बकस्तदा ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: ākramyorasi dakṣasya śitadhāreṇa hetinā chindannapi taduddhartuṃ nāśaknot tryambakastadā
translation: The three-eyed Vīrabhadra (threw down Dakṣa and) seating himself on Dakṣa’s chest, he tried to cut his head by a sharp-edged weapon, but he was unable to do so.

devanagari: शस्त्रैरस्त्रान्वितैरेवं अनिर्भिन्नत्वचं हरः । विस्मयं परमापन्नो दध्यौ पशुपतिश्चिरम् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: śastrairastrānvitairevaṃ anirbhinnatvacaṃ haraḥ vismayaṃ paramāpanno dadhyau paśupatiściram

translation: Hara (Vīrabhadra) was greatly wonder-struck to find that the skin of Dakṣa was not cut by weapons charmed with Vedic mantras called astras. He (who is a form of Paśupati) deeply thought what to do.

devanagari: दृष्ट्वा संज्ञपनं योगं पशूनां स पतिर्मखे । यजमानपशोः कस्य कायात्तेनाहरच्छिरः ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: dṛṣṭvā saṃjñapanaṃ yogaṃ paśūnāṃ sa patirmakhe yajamānapaśoḥ kasya kāyāttenāharacchiraḥ
translation: Observing the way in which animals are killed in the sacrifice, the Lord of Paśus (Souls) severed by that method the head of Dakṣa, the sacrificer, whom he made the animal to be sacrificed.

devanagari: साधुवादस्तदा तेषां कर्म तत्तस्य शंसताम् । भूतप्रेतपिशाचानां अन्येषां तद्विपर्ययः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: sādhuvādastadā teṣāṃ karma tattasya śaṃsatām bhūtapretapiśācānāṃ anyeṣāṃ tadviparyayaḥ
translation: There arose a shout of applause ‘well done’, ‘bravo’ from the Bhūtas, Pretas and Piśācas (members of Vīrabhadra’s army) who praised that act of gallantry. The reverse was the case in others (i.e. in Dakṣa’s camp).

devanagari: जुहावैतच्छिरस्तस्मिन् दक्षिणाग्नावमर्षितः । तद्देवयजनं दग्ध्वा प्रातिष्ठद् गुह्यकालयम् ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: juhāvaitacchirastasmin dakṣiṇāgnāvamarṣitaḥ taddevayajanaṃ dagdhvā prātiṣṭhad guhyakālayam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Full of rage, Vīrabhadra threw Dakṣa’s head in the sacrificial fire called Dakṣiṇāgni, as an oblation. Having burnt down the sacrificial edifice, he returned to Kailāsa, the home of Guhyakas.
